free_text: A study of the reaction C-12(O-16,a)Mg-24 below and
near the coulomb barrier.
.Beam intensities as high as 2.0 muA were
achieved, although the average was usually close to
1 muA.
Thin self-supported carbon foils, with thicknesses in
the range 10 to 25 mug/cm**2 were used as targets.
*By authors*.During the bombardment with O-16 beam the
target deteriorated in two ways-
*target thickness increased due to the cracking of
organic vapours on the target face.
*targets developed wrinkles, rips and tears after long
bombardment by the beam.
The product of the effective target thickness and
Beam intensity was monitored by measuring the O-16-C-12
elastic scattering.
Thin aluminium foils covering the detectors were used
to stop the heavy ions that were scattered and emitted
from  the target.
*By authors*.The absolute cross section scale was
determined from the O-16-C-12 Rutherford scattering at
the monitor detector angle and solid angles of
detectors.
